claim_error_rules.message: Claim has non-related op with main op code
claim_error_rules.meaning: One or more OP codes on the claim do not logically belong with the main operation code or are not valid supporting operations for the claimed repair.
likely_problem_areas: Causal part to op code relationship
validation_checks: Verify the main OP code is the correct primary repair operation.
Verify each additional OP code is allowed with the main OP code.
Check whether one of the listed OP codes belongs on a separate claim.
Confirm the causal part matches the main repair operation.
recommended_fix_steps: Open the returned claim and review all OP code lines.
Identify the intended main operation.
Remove unrelated labor operations or move them to a separate claim if appropriate.
Confirm the causal part supports the main operation.
Update the claim and resubmit.

claim_error_rules.message: Op Code not applicable to Causal Part
claim_error_rules.meaning: The selected OP code does not match the causal part entered on the claim.
validation_checks: Verify the causal part number is correct.
Verify the OP code belongs to the entered causal part.
Check whether the wrong OP code was selected.
Check whether the wrong causal part was entered.
recommended_fix_steps: Review the repair order and technician story.
Confirm the failed component that caused the repair.
Replace the incorrect causal part or incorrect OP code.
Save the corrected claim and validate again.

claim_error_rules.meaning: The OP code entered is not recognized, not valid for the claim type, or not valid in the current claim context.
validation_checks: Verify the OP code was typed correctly.
Verify the OP code is valid for warranty and not for a different claim type.
Verify the OP code is active and current.
Verify the labor operation belongs to the repair being claimed.
recommended_fix_steps: Compare the entered OP code against the repair source or campaign bulletin.
Correct any typing or formatting issue.
Replace the invalid code with the proper Hyundai labor operation.
Recalculate the claim and validate again.

claim_error_rules.message: *TT opcode not allowed as main OP
claim_error_rules.meaning: A time-trial, test, or auxiliary TT-type OP code was entered as the primary repair operation when it must be used only as a supporting operation.
validation_checks: Verify whether the TT code is intended as a supporting operation only.
Verify a valid primary repair operation exists.
Check whether the main repair line was omitted.
recommended_fix_steps: Replace the TT code as the main operation with the correct repair OP code.
Move the TT code to a supporting line if allowed.
Confirm labor hours and quantities still match the repair.
Save and validate the claim again.

claim_error_rules.message: Rental or support claim missing related repaired vehicle claim linkage
claim_error_rules.meaning: The rental claim is missing the related repaired vehicle claim reference or supporting repair order linkage.
validation_checks: Verify the repaired vehicle claim number is present if required.
Verify the repair order number matches the related repair event.
Verify VIN and mileage belong to the repaired vehicle.
Confirm the rental reason ties to a valid repair event.
recommended_fix_steps: Open the rental claim and review the repaired vehicle section.
Enter or correct the related repair order and claim reference.
Confirm VIN, mileage, and causal part data are tied to the repaired vehicle.
Attach any missing rental support documents and resubmit.

claim_error_rules.message: Required attachment missing or not categorized correctly
claim_error_rules.meaning: A required file is missing, not selected, or uploaded under the wrong category.
validation_checks: Verify required images or documents are attached.
Verify each file uses the correct attachment category.
Check whether VIN, Odometer/Mileage, Part, or Sublet support is missing when required.
Check whether the file size exceeds the allowed upload limit.
recommended_fix_steps: Review the claim type and required attachment categories.
Upload missing files or correct the category assignment.
Confirm the files are clear, complete, and tied to the correct VIN/repair event.
Resave and resubmit the claim.

claim_error_rules.message: One or more required fields are missing
claim_error_rules.meaning: A mandatory field required for claim entry or resubmission has not been completed.
validation_checks: Verify all required Basic Information fields are completed.
Verify required complaint, cause, and correction descriptions are entered.
Verify required OP code or causal part fields are present.
Verify dates and mileage fields are not blank.
recommended_fix_steps: Review the highlighted required fields on the screen.
Complete all missing required entries.
Validate character limits and restricted character rules where applicable.
Save and submit again.

claim_error_rules.message: Restricted characters used in text entry field
claim_error_rules.meaning: One or more text fields contain characters that the Hyundai portal does not allow.
validation_checks: Check for forbidden punctuation and symbols in entered text.
Review copied and pasted notes for hidden or unsupported characters.
Confirm the text stays within the field character limit.
recommended_fix_steps: Remove restricted characters from the affected text fields.
Use plain text phrasing only.
Save the claim and validate again.

claim_error_rules.message: Claim submission or resubmission over 30 days is subject to denial
claim_error_rules.meaning: The claim is being submitted or resubmitted outside Hyundai’s preferred timing window and may be denied.
validation_checks: Compare repair date and current submission date.
Check how long the claim has been in return or pending status.
Confirm whether supporting explanation is needed.
recommended_fix_steps: Review the dates tied to the original repair and return.
Correct the claim promptly.
Provide any needed dealer reply or explanation if applicable.
Resubmit without further delay.

claim_error_rules.message: Claim in hold status because corresponding repair claim was not found
claim_error_rules.meaning: The system could not match the submitted data to the expected repair claim record, often due to incorrect RO number, claim number, or related identifiers.
validation_checks: Verify the repair order number is correct.
Verify the claim number is correct.
Verify the causal part and repair linkage fields match the related repair event.
Check whether the referenced repair claim exists in the system.
recommended_fix_steps: Review the returned claim carefully against the original repair order.
Correct any mismatched identifiers.
Update related claim linkage if required.
Use Dealer PWA Reply if needed to explain the correction.
Resubmit after confirming the repair record can be matched.

claim_error_rules.meaning: An uploaded file exceeds the maximum size allowed by the claim screen.
validation_checks: Check the file size against the screen limit.
Confirm the correct claim type is being used, since limits differ by claim type.
Verify the attachment is still readable after resizing if adjusted.
recommended_fix_steps: Reduce the file size or replace the file with a smaller version.
Re-upload the attachment under the correct category.
Confirm the attachment remains legible and complete.

claim_error_rules.message: PDI claim missing required R.O. and VIN line information
claim_error_rules.meaning: The PDI claim is missing one or more required values in the R.O. and VIN table.
validation_checks: Verify R.O. # is entered.
Verify VIN is entered.
Verify mileage in and mileage out are entered.
Verify R/O date is entered.
recommended_fix_steps: Complete the missing R.O./VIN row fields.
Review the PDI line for correct vehicle and mileage data.
Save and submit again.

claim_error_rules.meaning: A campaign claim cannot be processed without the proper campaign operation code.
validation_checks: Verify the campaign OP code is present.
Verify the OP code matches the campaign being claimed.
Verify VIN and campaign repair context align.
recommended_fix_steps: Enter the correct campaign OP code.
Verify the campaign description is correct.
Save and submit the campaign claim again.
